item.title: Tankers transiting Strait of Hormuz stop or turn around amid US blockade, data shows
item.description: US military says no ships pass through its operation to seal off Iranian ports in first 24 hours

item.title: Kevin Warsh is very rich, or possibly very, very rich
item.description: In the Fed with the bread

item.title: Musk is officially free to roam in Texas
item.description: Delaware ruling draws a line under Tesla’s corporate domicile in the Lone Star State

item.title: How the US can blockade the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US forces will be ready to descend from helicopters on to oil tankers

item.title: Wall Street banks break records as Iran war drives trading boom
item.description: JPMorgan Chase, Citigroup and Wells Fargo reported more than $25bn of profits for the first quarter

item.title: Ad giant Publicis is less ‘Mad Men’, more ‘The IT Crowd’
item.description: Investors are not buying the French group’s AI-fuelled growth story

item.title: The US is at risk of an oil shock too
item.description: In a global market for crude, Asian buyers are vacuuming up supplies, raising prices for Europe and America

item.title: South East Water boss waives bonus after outages
item.description: David Hinton had previously blamed shortages on rise in people working from home

item.title: BlackRock profits jump as it draws in $130bn
item.description: Earnings for first quarter underline asset manager’s push into investment products that generate higher fees

item.title: Gucci sales slump deals blow to De Meo’s Kering turnaround
item.description: Sales at luxury group’s key brand tumbled 8% in the first quarter as Iran war takes toll on industry

item.title: Fuel price protests spread to roads across Northern Ireland
item.description: Cash-strapped Stormont executive appeals to prime minister for aid package for hard-pressed region

item.title: UK regulator bans unauthorised Martin Lewis ads for car finance claims
item.description: Conclusive Financial’s promotions boasted of high compensation figures and used images without permission

item.title: Latham partners pocket record $8.7mn average pay
item.description: US law firm’s revenues surpass $8bn for first time on the back of megamerger mandates

item.title: Iran war could slow global growth to weakest since pandemic, IMF warns
item.description: Expansion of global economy would fall to 2.5% in an ‘adverse scenario’ of persistent $100-per-barrel oil

item.title: Amazon agrees $11.6bn takeover of satellite group Globalstar
item.description: Deal will bolster tech group’s efforts to challenge Elon Musk’s Starlink with its own low Earth orbit satellite business

item.title: BP’s new chief executive to overhaul company into two units
item.description: Meg O’Neill plans to return BP to structure before its complicated 2020 reorganisation

item.title: Global oil demand plummets by most since pandemic, says IEA
item.description: Demand drops 3.4% in March due to soaring prices, supply shortages and collapse of Middle East air travel

item.title: Rolls-Royce targets 100 ultra-wealthy buyers with customised electric cars
item.description: UK carmaker tests limits of the market with its personalised vehicles for the super-rich

item.title: Axel Springer’s Telegraph deal waved through by UK government
item.description: Culture secretary’s decision not to refer bid to watchdogs means end of three-year sales process is in sight

item.title: PwC plans overhaul of global consulting business
item.description: Shake-up spurred on by rise of AI and threat of major upheaval across the industry

item.title: Europe’s carbon pricing scheme under scrutiny as energy costs soar
item.description: The cost of polluting in Europe and the UK has fallen since the start of 2026

item.title: Merck transforms itself through dealmaking as clock ticks on top-selling drug
item.description: Acquisitions fuel pharma group’s evolution as it prepares to lose patent protection on cancer drug Keytruda

item.title: Investors set to scrutinise exposure to private credit as Wall Street reports earnings
item.description: First-quarter results come amid growing concerns around the links between traditional lenders and the private credit boom

item.title: FirstFT: Predictions of an ‘exceptionally strong’ earnings season
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Iran and the US keep talking while OpenAI’s $852bn valuation comes under scrutiny from backers

item.title: ECB seeks to break ‘deadlock’ on common deposit insurance
item.description: Regulator urges governments to fast-track long-delayed idea to help break down merger barriers

item.title: Wallenbergs rescue Swedish green steel project with €1.4bn funding deal
item.description: Stegra has been striving to avoid same fate as bankrupt Northvolt

item.title: LVMH sales hit as Middle East conflict delays luxury recovery
item.description: US and Israel’s war on Iran dents sales growth at world’s biggest luxury group

item.title: BP hails ‘exceptional’ quarter for oil traders as Iran war stokes volatility
item.description: Energy shock unleashed by the conflict has left traders and refineries competing for available cargoes

item.title: Hong Kong should ditch Cantonese for Mandarin, says Shangri-La scion
item.description: Changing the medium of instruction in schools would boost competitiveness, according to hotel group chief Hui Kuok

item.title: Small caps revisited
item.description: Index choice matters, and so do valuations
